artifacts.sourceTitle: Using Artificial Intelligence | Student Community Standards | Office of the Dean of Students | Oregon State University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At OSU, individual faculty have the freedom to set course-specific expectations for artificial intelligence use. It is important to recognize that different classes may have widely varying or even contradictory expectations. You must follow the specific expectations for each course.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow candidate: OSU lists recommended and approved GenAI tools and data classifications; not treated as a public canonical claim.
Approved as directly supported by the OSU AI guidelines page.
Approved because the CTL guidance directly says instructors may not input student work into non-approved GenAI tools.
Approved with wording that preserves strongly encouraged rather than must or required.
Approved because the Student Community Standards source directly links misuse to possible Academic Integrity Policy violations.
Approved as student-facing guidance scoped to course-specific expectations rather than a uniform AI policy.
Approved as Ecampus faculty guidance and worded as recommendation, not binding university-wide policy.

artifacts.title: Oregon State University AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official OSU sources provide central guidance on recommended and approved GenAI tools, data classification limits, instructor handling of student work in GenAI tools, strongly encouraged GenAI syllabus statements, student-facing academic integrity expectations, and Ecampus assignment-level AI guidance.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
College-specific, blog-only, and login-gated sources were not used for claim extraction.
Ecampus evidence is scoped as faculty guidance and not a university-wide binding policy.
